dataIdInfo.idAbs:
The Pierce County Equity Index data highlights opportunities to improve equitable access and outcomes for residents of Pierce County. This Index includes an overall Opportunity Index rating which is made up of five categories (Livability, Accessibility, Economy, Education, and Environmental Health), and 32 individual data points. The data is presented in the Pierce County Equity Index web application (www.piercecountywa.gov/equityindex).

Accessibility Indicators: Average Road Quality, Transit, Internet and Library Access, Parks & Open Spaces, Voter Participation, Retail Services, Household Vehicle Access and Healthily Food Availability.

Education Indicators: High School Graduation Rate, 25 Age+ with Bachelors' Degree or More, Average Test Proficiency, Average Student Mobility Rate, Kindergarten Readiness Rate.

Economy Indicators: Households at 200% of the Poverty Line or Less, Median Household Income, Jobs, Unemployment Rate, Poverty Rate, Median Home Value.

Livability Indicators: Cost Burden, Life Expectancy, Health, Uninsured rate, Crime, Crashes

Environmental Health Indicators: NOxNOx- Diesel Emissions (Annual Tons/Km2), Ozone Concentration, PM2.5 Particulate Matter Concentration, Populations Near Heavy Traffic Roadways.

Please read metadata for additional information (https://matterhorn.co.pierce.wa.us/GISmetadata/pdbis_equityindex.html). Any use or data download constitutes acceptance of the Terms of Use (https://matterhorn.co.pierce.wa.us/Disclaimer/PierceCountyGISDataTermsofUse.pdf).
dataIdInfo.idPurp: The Pierce County Equity Index data highlights opportunities to improve equitable access and outcomes for residents of Pierce County.
dataIdInfo.idCredit: Pierce County and the Ohio State University Kirwan Institute of Race and Social Justice.

attr.attrdef: The unique 12-digit FIPS Code. FIPS codes are used to identify geographic regions like states, counties, census tract, and block group.
The unique 12-digit FIPS Code. FIPS codes are used to identify geographic regions like states, counties, census tract, and block group.
Count of persons living within the area.
The total number of households in a block group. It is used for the the household poverty rate.
Total number of households that make less and 20k as an estimate of households below the federal poverty level
The rate of household poverty. Households that make less than 20K divided by the total number of households (pov\popForPov).

Percent of population within the block group earning less than the Federal Poverty Limit (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Lower poverty rates show better access to living-wage jobs and economic stability.

Median home value under 200K in a block group (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Higher median home prices contribute to homeowner wealth accumulation and reductions in housing affordability among residents.

Median home value between 200K and 300K in a block group (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Higher median home prices contribute to homeowner wealth accumulation and reductions in housing affordability among residents.

Median home value over 300K in a block group (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Higher median home prices contribute to homeowner wealth accumulation and reductions in housing affordability among residents.
Percent of owner-occupied units used for the Home Owership Rate (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Homeownership is correlated with building wealth and increased stability.

Percentage of owners paying more than 30% of income on a mortgage (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Households with a lower cost burden are more likely to accommodate unexpected financial expenditures.

Percentage of renters paying more than 30% of income in rent (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Housing cost-burden is a barrier to opportunity. This burden indicates that a household may not be able to afford other critical and nondiscretionary costs such as health, food, and transportation.
Population 25 Plus is used in calculating the Educational Attainment Index (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Education can increase self-esteem, workforce skills, and financial stability.
Adults (25 plus) who do not finish highschool (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Education can increase self-esteem, workforce skills, and financial stability.
Adults (25 plus) who complete High School or attain a GEM (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Education can increase self-esteem, workforce skills, and financial stability.
Adults (25 plus) who complete some College (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Education can increase self-esteem, workforce skills, and financial stability.
Adults (25 plus) who attain an Associate's Degree (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Education can increase self-esteem, workforce skills, and financial stability.
Adults (25 plus) who attain an Bachler's Degree (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Education can increase self-esteem, workforce skills, and financial stability.
Adults (25 plus) who attain an Master's Degree or higher (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Education can increase self-esteem, workforce skills, and financial stability.
Adults (25 plus) who reported the level of education achieved (2020, Block Group). Why Include this Metric? Education can increase self-esteem, workforce skills, and financial stability.

Number of people who only speak English, used to calculate Limited English Rate (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Communities with high levels of linguistic isolation experience greater civil engagement barriers and is an indicator of social vulnerability.

Population over 5 years old, used to calculate Limited English Rate (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Communities with high levels of linguistic isolation experience greater civil engagement barriers and is an indicator of social vulnerability.

Population over 5 years old who speak limited English, used to calculate Limited English Rate (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Communities with high levels of linguistic isolation experience greater civil engagement barriers and is an indicator of social vulnerability.

Percent of population with limited English proficiency (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Communities with high levels of linguistic isolation experience greater civil engagement barriers and is an indicator of social vulnerability.
Total female population per block group (2020).
Total male population per block group (2020).
Total male population under 20 years old per block group (2020).
Total female population under 20 years old per block group (2020).
Total male population between 20 and 29 years old per block group (2020).
Total female population between 20 and 29 years old per block group (2020).
Total male population between 30 and 44 years old per block group (2020).
Total male population between 30 and 44 years old per block group (2020).
Total male population between 45 and 64 years old per block group (2020).
Total female population between 45 and 64 years old per block group (2020).
Total male population over 65 years old per block group (2020).
Total female population over 65 years old per block group (2020).

Number of households with income over 200K in a given area (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Household income provides a picture of the standard of living of various households. It is also a good indicator of the local economies.

Number of households with income between 100K and 200K in a given area (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Household income provides a picture of the standard of living of various households. It is also a good indicator of the local economies.

Number of households with income between 75K and 100K in a given area (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Household income provides a picture of the standard of living of various households. It is also a good indicator of the local economies.

Number of households with income between 50K and 75K in a given area (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Household income provides a picture of the standard of living of various households. It is also a good indicator of the local economies.

Number of households with income between 35K and 50K in a given area (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Household income provides a picture of the standard of living of various households. It is also a good indicator of the local economies.

Number of households with income between 20K and 35K in a given area (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Household income provides a picture of the standard of living of various households. It is also a good indicator of the local economies.

Number of households with income under 20K in a given area (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Household income provides a picture of the standard of living of various households. It is also a good indicator of the local economies.

Total number of households in a given area, it is used to calculate the percent of households in each income range (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Household income provides a picture of the standard of living of various households. It is also a good indicator of the local economies.
Number of whites with Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of blacks with Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of Native American with Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of Asian with Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of Pacific Islander individuals with Hispanic or Latino decent in a given block group (2020).
Number of Other race individuals with Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of multi-race individuals with Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of whites with Non-Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of blacks with Non-Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of Native Americans with Non-Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of Asians with Non-Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of Pacific Islanders with Non-Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of other race individuals with Non-Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).
Number of Multi-race individuals with Non-Hispanic or Latino heritage in a given block group (2020).

Percent of block group within 10 miles of regional parks (2022, block group). Why include this metric? Access to parks and open space can encourage increased physical activity, improved mental health, and community connectedness.

Percent of block group within 1 mile of community parks (2022, block group). Why include this metric? Access to parks and open space can encourage increased physical activity, improved mental health, and community connectedness.

Percent of block group within 1/2 mile of neighborhood parks (2022, block group). Why include this metric? Access to parks and open space can encourage increased physical activity, improved mental health, and community connectedness.

Percentage of a block group's area within 1/2 mile of neighborhood parks, 1 mile of community parks, and 10 miles of regional parks divided by total block group area. Overlapping park areas yield higher totals, meaning greater park access. Park classifications per PSRC urban open space plan.

The percent of voters who voted in the general election divided by the number of eligible voters in the block group for the year 2020. This evaluates voter participation and can be used to determine access to civic processes. Why include this metric? Voting is a significant measure of participation in civic life and captures both the barriers to and the level of civic engagement.

Aggregates the average quality of roads in a block group. Lower scores indicate poorer road quality resulting in higher costs of repair to the local municipality (2019, Block Group). Why include this Metric? Maintenance of the road system supports access and community connection.
attr.attrdefs: Pierce County Road Conditions provided by Pierce County Planning and Public Works, City of Tacoma Department of Public Works

attr.attrdef: The percentage of households with access to home internet (2020, Block Group). Why include this metric? Higher percentages indicate higher access to internet usage which is beneficial to families and children.

Percentage of a block group's area within 1/4 mile of rail and bus service stops, within 3 miles of park and ride locations, and within micro transit areas from Pierce County Transit and Paratransit service divided by total block group area. Overlapping transit areas yield higher totals, meaning greater transit access. Why include this metric? Transit options increase access to essential services, amenities and employment.

Percent of block group within 1/2 mile of library in urban area and 10 miles of library in rural area (2020). Why include this metric? Libraries increase access to information, resources, and technology

Total number of employees by place of work per block group in retail service categories. When multiple block groups are selected, the sum of the employees is shown (2020). Why include this metric? Retail services that are close to communities provide needed services and support the local economy.

Percentage of block group population with access to a household vehicle (2020). Why include this metric? Access to a household vehicle increases mobility, particularly in rural areas, where transit service is less frequent.
All crime reports counted by block group in Pierce County (2022).
attr.attrdefs: Pierce County Crime Data, Tacoma Crime Extract

attr.attrdef: Count of property crimes within the past two years, including theft, vehicle theft, and vandalism (2020-2022). Why include this metric? Crime affects overall perceptions of safety, social connection, and economic opportunity.
attr.attrdefs: Pierce County Crime Data, Tacoma Crime Extract

attr.attrdef: Count of personal crimes within the past two years, including offenses such assault, rape and murder (2020 - 2022). Why include this metric? Crime affects overall perceptions of safety, social connection, and economic opportunity.
attr.attrdefs: Pierce County Crime Data, Tacoma Crime Extract

attr.attrdef: The total percentage of all owners & renters in a census block group spending 30% or more of their income on housing costs (2020). Why include this metric? Households with a lower cost burden are more likely to accommodate unexpected financial expenditures.

Average Life Expectancy of those residing within a particular census tract. Seen as a bellwether indicator on health conditions in a particular neighborhood (2015). Why include this metric? Life expectancy reflects physical health and access to healthcare.
attr.attrdefs: US Small-area Life Expectancy Estimates Project – 2010-2015 Averages

attr.attrdef: Percent of block group CDC survey participants who responded that their health is "fair" or "poor"(2021). Why include this metric? Self-rated health reflects individuals' perception of their health and access to necessary care.
Percent of a block group without health insurance (2020). Why include this metric? Higher values represent block group's with greater disparities and less access to health care services.

Serious or fatal crashes in last two years involving bicyclists or pedestrians (2021). Why include this metric? Access to safe roads by pedestrians and cyclists increases mobility, community connection, and physical activity.

Weighted average index educational attainment in population 25+ with: 1*associates degree + 1.2*bachelor's degree + 1.5* master's + PhD degree (2020). Why include this metric? Education can increase self-esteem, workforce skills, and financial stability.

Combined average of 3rd grade reading and 7th grade math scores. Since Pierce County does not have attendance zones, the method for allocating school statistics to block groups was based on the distance of block group centroid to a school. The three closest elementary and middle schools to each block group were used (2020). Why include this metric? Testing proficiency shows what students know and their school’s success in educating them.

The percentage of students who enroll in the beginning of the school year but did not stay enrolled in the same school throughout that school year (continuity rate). Since Pierce County does not have attendance zones, the method for allocating school statistics to block groups was based on the distance of block group centroid to a school. The three closest elementary, middle, and high schools to each block group were used (2021). Why include this Metric? School systems with higher student mobility have more challenges.

The 4-year adjusted cohort graduation rate. This is the number of students who completed high school in four years, divided by the number of students who are in their "cohort"; those that started 9th grade at the same time. "Adjusted" refers to the fact that some students may have moved in or out of the school, and the cohort size needs to be adjusted to accurately track those students. Since Pierce County does not have attendance zones, the method for allocating school statistics to block groups was based on the distance of block group centroid to a school. The three closest high schools to each block group were used (2021). Why include this Metric? Higher high school graduation rate correlates to greater access to opportunity.

Percent of students entering Kindergarten that achieve at least 5 out of 6 readiness measures. Since Pierce County does not have attendance zones, the method for allocating school statistics to block groups was based on the distance of block group centroid to a school. The three closest elementary schools to each block group were used (2021). Why include this metric? Kindergarten readiness relates to educational success later in life.

A weighted jobs index looking at three wage categories: 1) Less than $15,000, 2) $15,000 - $40,000, 3) More than $40,000. Takes total numbers of jobs in each category and scores higher paying jobs more. (2019, Block Group) Why Include this Metric? Access to higher-paying jobs increases financial stability, housing options, and wealth accumulation.
attr.attrdefs: Longitudinal Employer-Household Dynamics (LEHD) On The Map, All Jobs & Earnings

attr.attrdef: Percent of individuals within block group earning less than 200% of Federal Poverty Level (2020). Why include this metric? Lower poverty rates show better access to living-wage jobs and economic stability.

The median household income in a given area (2020). Why include this metric? Household income provides a picture of the standard of living of various households. It is also a good indicator of the local economies.

Unemployed percentage within a block group of the total eligible workforce over 16 years of age (2020). Why include this metric? Unemployment can limit access to necessities like healthcare and housing.
Owner occupied home ownership rate by block group (2020). Why include this metric? Homeownership is correlated with building wealth and increased stability.

Median home value in a block group of all owner occupied housing units (2020). Why include this Metric? Higher median home prices contribute to homeowner wealth accumulation and reductions in housing affordability among residents.

Diesel and Nitric Oxide emissions average for the area (Annual Tons/Km2), estimated by EPA modeling (2016). Why include this metric? Lower diesel emissions lead to reduced rates of asthma and respiratory illness, eye and skin irritation, and heart and lung disease.

Ground level ozone concentration, as estimated by EPA (2017). Why include this metric? Lower surface ozone concentration leads to reduced rates of asthma and respiratory illness, eye and skin irritation, and heart and lung disease.

Concentration of fine particulate matter estimated based on air quality monitors and atmospheric circulation and dispersion models from the US EPA (2017). Why include this metric? Less exposure to particulate matter leads to reduced rates of asthma and respiratory illness, eye and skin irritation, and heart and lung disease.

Number of people within a block group in proximity to high traffic roadways (2019). Why include this metric? High traffic roadways are correlated with higher incidence of certain types of disease and pollutants.

The range of the average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Accessibility Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? Greater community access to input indicators can support economic, mental and physical, and environmental well-being. This index can help prioritize which communities need more support in relation to accessibility.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: Average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Accessibility Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? Greater community access to input indicators can support economic, mental and physical, and environmental well-being. This index can help prioritize which communities need more support in relation to accessibility.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: The range of the average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Economy Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? Economic health affects all aspects of a community. A lower score indicates communities in need of economic support, such as more jobs and higher wages.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: Average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Economy Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? Economic health affects all aspects of a community. A lower score indicates communities in need of economic support, such as more jobs and higher wages.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: The range of the average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Education Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? Education is a major factor in economic development, quality of life, and well-being. A lower score indicates communities in need of education support, such as greater funding and system improvements.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: Average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Education Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? Education is a major factor in economic development, quality of life, and well-being. A lower score indicates communities in need of education support, such as greater funding and system improvements.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: The range of the average of average Z-Scores for the Accessibility, Livability, Education, Economic, and Environment Indexes over the area of analysis to obtain final equity index using text definitions. Why include this metric? Text definitions make for easier interpretation of the index. The very low definition is indicative of overall lower community conditions based on accessibility, livability, education, economy, and environment indexes compared to the very high definition.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: Average of average Z-Scores for the Accessibility, Livability, Education, Economic, and Environment Indexes over the area of analysis to obtain final equity index using text definitions.

The range of the average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Environment Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? The environmental quality of a community gives insight into the relationship between the environment and a community's health. A lower score indicates communities with low environmental health, highlighting environmental justice issues, exposures, and adverse health outcomes.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: Average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Environment Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? The environmental quality of a community gives insight into the relationship between the environment and a community's health. A lower score indicates communities with low environmental health, highlighting environmental justice issues, exposures, and adverse health outcomes.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: The range of the average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Livability Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? The livability index gives insight into general quality of life and how livable an area is. This index can help prioritize which communities need more amenities and services to help improve the quality of life.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: Average of Z-Scores of each of the indicators in the Livability Group over the area of analysis. Why include this metric? The livability index gives insight into general quality of life and how livable an area is. This index can help prioritize which communities need more amenities and services to help improve the quality of life.
attr.attrdefs: Calculated by the CAI web application

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.
Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.
